NR-AhR.label: Aryl hydrocarbon receptor
NR-AhR.desc: A ligand-activated transcription factor. Activation induces CYP1A1/1B1 metabolism genes and is associated with dioxin-like toxicity and immunosuppression.

NR-AR.label: Androgen receptor (full length)
NR-AR.desc: Steroid hormone receptor mediating male sex hormone signalling. Disruption linked to reproductive toxicity and endocrine interference.

NR-AR-LBD.label: Androgen receptor ligand-binding domain
NR-AR-LBD.desc: Binding assay for the AR ligand-binding domain only. Compounds active here but not in the full-length assay may act through other receptor regions.

NR-AhR-LBD.label: Aryl hydrocarbon receptor (LBD)
NR-AhR-LBD.desc: Ligand-binding domain variant of the AhR assay. Used to identify direct ligands vs indirect activators.

NR-ER.label: Estrogen receptor alpha (full length)
NR-ER.desc: Key estrogen receptor subtype. Agonists may mimic estrogen in vivo, raising concerns about reproductive and developmental toxicity.

NR-ER-LBD.label: Estrogen receptor alpha (LBD)
NR-ER-LBD.desc: Ligand-binding domain binding assay for ERα. Identifies direct ER ligands with potential estrogenic activity.

NR-PPAR-gamma.label: Peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor gamma
NR-PPAR-gamma.desc: Nuclear receptor regulating adipogenesis and glucose metabolism. Activation may indicate metabolic disruption; also a target for thiazolidinedione drugs.

SR-ARE.label: Antioxidant response element
SR-ARE.desc: Activated by oxidative or electrophilic stress via Nrf2. Induction signals cellular stress but also cytoprotective responses.

SR-ATAD5.label: ATAD5 genotoxicity marker
SR-ATAD5.desc: Upregulation of ATAD5 (a DNA repair regulator) indicates genotoxic stress. Used as a surrogate for DNA damage potential.

SR-HSE.label: Heat shock element stress response
SR-HSE.desc: Induced by proteotoxic stress that leads to protein misfolding. A broad marker for cytotoxic cellular stress.

SR-MMP.label: Mitochondrial membrane potential
SR-MMP.desc: Loss of MMP indicates mitochondrial dysfunction. Compounds active here may cause energy depletion and apoptosis.

SR-p53.label: p53 tumour suppressor pathway
SR-p53.desc: Activation of p53 signalling indicates DNA damage or genotoxic stress. Relevant to mutagenicity and carcinogenicity assessment.

FDA_APPROVED.label: FDA approved drug
FDA_APPROVED.desc: Compound appears in the FDA approved drug list. Provides positive context — approved drugs have passed clinical safety and efficacy review.

CT_TOX.label: Clinical trial toxicity failure
CT_TOX.desc: Compound failed clinical trials due to toxicity reasons. High probability here is a strong adverse signal based on real-world clinical data.

Hepatobiliary disorders.label: Hepatobiliary disorders
Hepatobiliary disorders.desc: Adverse events affecting the liver and bile duct reported in drug labelling. Includes hepatotoxicity, cholestasis, and liver enzyme elevations.

Metabolism and nutrition disorders.label: Metabolism and nutrition disorders
Metabolism and nutrition disorders.desc: Side effects related to metabolic processes — weight changes, hyperglycaemia, dyslipidaemia, and electrolyte imbalances.

Product issues.label: Product issues
Product issues.desc: Adverse events related to the drug product itself (formulation, stability) rather than the active molecule.

Eye disorders.label: Eye disorders
Eye disorders.desc: Ocular adverse effects including vision changes, retinopathy, and drug-induced eye inflammation.

Investigations.label: Laboratory investigations
Investigations.desc: Abnormal clinical or laboratory findings reported as adverse events, such as elevated enzymes or abnormal ECG.

Musculoskeletal and connective tissue di.label: Musculoskeletal disorders
Musculoskeletal and connective tissue di.desc: Side effects affecting muscles, joints, and connective tissue. Includes myalgia, arthralgia, and rhabdomyolysis.

Gastrointestinal disorders.label: Gastrointestinal disorders
Gastrointestinal disorders.desc: GI side effects including nausea, vomiting, diarrhoea, and abdominal pain — among the most commonly reported drug adverse events.

Social circumstances.label: Social circumstances
Social circumstances.desc: Social or lifestyle-related outcomes reported in drug labelling, such as substance use or social functioning changes.

Immune system disorders.label: Immune system disorders
Immune system disorders.desc: Adverse events related to immune activation or suppression, including hypersensitivity, allergic reactions, and autoimmune effects.

Reproductive system and breast disorders.label: Reproductive system disorders
Reproductive system and breast disorders.desc: Side effects affecting the reproductive system — menstrual irregularities, fertility changes, and sexual dysfunction.

Neoplasms benign, malignant and unspecif.label: Neoplasms
Neoplasms benign, malignant and unspecif.desc: Drug-associated tumour or neoplasm formation reported in clinical or post-marketing data.

Psychiatric disorders.label: Psychiatric disorders
Psychiatric disorders.desc: CNS psychiatric adverse effects including depression, anxiety, psychosis, and mood changes.

Renal and urinary disorders.label: Renal and urinary disorders
Renal and urinary disorders.desc: Kidney and urinary tract side effects including nephrotoxicity, elevated creatinine, and urinary retention.

Pregnancy, puerperium and perinatal cond.label: Pregnancy and perinatal effects
Pregnancy, puerperium and perinatal cond.desc: Adverse effects during pregnancy or the perinatal period, including teratogenicity and effects on foetal development.

Ear and labyrinth disorders.label: Ear and labyrinth disorders
Ear and labyrinth disorders.desc: Auditory and vestibular side effects including ototoxicity, tinnitus, and hearing loss.

Cardiac disorders.label: Cardiac disorders
Cardiac disorders.desc: Cardiotoxic adverse effects including QT prolongation, arrhythmia, myocardial infarction, and heart failure.

Vascular disorders.label: Vascular disorders
Vascular disorders.desc: Adverse effects on blood vessels including hypertension, hypotension, thromboembolism, and vasculitis.

Blood and lymphatic system disorders.label: Blood and lymphatic disorders
Blood and lymphatic system disorders.desc: Haematological side effects including anaemia, thrombocytopenia, leucopenia, and coagulation abnormalities.

Skin and subcutaneous tissue disorders.label: Skin and subcutaneous disorders
Skin and subcutaneous tissue disorders.desc: Dermatological adverse effects including rash, Stevens-Johnson syndrome, photosensitivity, and alopecia.

Congenital, familial and genetic disorde.label: Congenital and genetic disorders
Congenital, familial and genetic disorde.desc: Adverse events related to genetic or developmental abnormalities — often associated with teratogenic compounds.

Infections and infestations.label: Infections and infestations
Infections and infestations.desc: Increased risk of infection, often due to immunosuppressive drug effects. Includes opportunistic infections.

Respiratory, thoracic and mediastinal di.label: Respiratory disorders
Respiratory, thoracic and mediastinal di.desc: Pulmonary adverse effects including dyspnoea, cough, pulmonary fibrosis, and bronchospasm.

Surgical and medical procedures.label: Surgical and medical procedures
Surgical and medical procedures.desc: Adverse outcomes related to procedures that may be associated with the drug's use or its management.

Endocrine disorders.label: Endocrine disorders
Endocrine disorders.desc: Side effects affecting hormone-secreting glands — thyroid dysfunction, adrenal suppression, and diabetes-related effects.

General disorders and administration sit.label: General disorders
General disorders and administration sit.desc: Non-specific adverse effects including fatigue, fever, oedema, and pain at the administration site.

Nervous system disorders.label: Nervous system disorders
Nervous system disorders.desc: CNS and peripheral nervous system adverse effects including headache, neuropathy, seizures, and cognitive changes.

CYP1A2.label: CYP1A2 inhibition
CYP1A2.desc: Inhibition of cytochrome P450 1A2 — a key drug-drug interaction enzyme involved in caffeine, clozapine, and theophylline metabolism.

CYP2C9.label: CYP2C9 inhibition
CYP2C9.desc: Inhibition of CYP2C9, which metabolises ~15% of clinically used drugs including warfarin, phenytoin, and NSAIDs.

CYP2C19.label: CYP2C19 inhibition
CYP2C19.desc: Inhibition of CYP2C19, which metabolises proton pump inhibitors, clopidogrel, and several antidepressants. Highly polymorphic in humans.

CYP2D6.label: CYP2D6 inhibition
CYP2D6.desc: Inhibition of CYP2D6, responsible for ~25% of drug metabolism including opioids, antipsychotics, and beta-blockers. Polymorphic.

CYP3A4.label: CYP3A4 inhibition
CYP3A4.desc: Inhibition of CYP3A4, the most abundant hepatic CYP enzyme, responsible for ~50% of drug metabolism and a major DDI risk.

mutagenic.label: Ames mutagenicity
mutagenic.desc: Positive Ames test result — indicates potential genotoxicity via frameshift or base-pair substitution mutations. Standard early-stage safety screen.

p_np.label: BBB penetration
p_np.desc: Blood-brain barrier permeability (positive = penetrates CNS). Critical for CNS drug design; also relevant to CNS side-effect risk.

HIV_active.label: HIV activity
HIV_active.desc: Inhibits HIV replication in a cell-based assay. Positive indicates antiviral activity against the HIV-1 strain.
